name: Maple Apple Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt.
3. Cut in Crisco shortening with pastry blender or 2 knives until mixture is uniform.
4. Sprinkle 7 tablespoons cold water in, 1 tablespoon at a time, until dough forms a ball.
5. Divide dough into 2 equal parts.
6. On lightly floured surface, roll bottom crust into circle 1/8 inch thick and 1/2 inch larger than inverted 9-inch pie plate.
7. Gently ease crust into greased plate, being careful not to stretch the dough.
8. Trim edge even with plate.
9. Microwave apple slices on high for 2 minutes. Stir; repeat procedure twice or until apples begin to soften. Set aside.
10. In a separate microwaving bowl, combine maple syrup and cornstarch. Microwave for 2 minutes; stir. Repeat procedure until mixture starts to thicken.
11. Add the thickened mixture to apples. Toss lightly until apples are well coated. Set aside to cool.
12. Spoon cooled apple filling into unbaked pastry shell.
13. For nut filling, combine pecans, maple syrup, and butter in a microwaving bowl. Microwave on high for 1 minute, stirring, then continue to microwave an additional minute on medium setting. Stir to break up mixture.
14. Spread nut mixture on top of apple filling.
15. On lightly floured surface, roll out top crust the same as bottom.
16. Carefully lift top crust onto filled pie. Trim 1/2 inch beyond edge of pie plate.
17. Fold top edge under crust. Crimp edges or flute as desired.
18. Slit top crust with knife to allow steam to escape.
19. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 60 minutes, or until apples start to bubble.
20. Brush top of pie with maple syrup. Continue to bake an additional 5 minutes.
